pattern: Interval DP decides which substrings can vanish entirely, then a suffix DP keeps only the deletions that lower the surviving string.
hints: Two letters cancel when they are neighbors in the circular alphabet, and after a cancellation new neighbors can appear, so think recursively about whole removable blocks.
Precompute, for every substring, whether it can be deleted completely: pair its first character with a matching later one whose enclosed part also deletes, leaving the remainder deletable too.
With that table, scan from each position choosing either to keep the current character or to erase a removable block starting there, and take the lexicographically smaller continuation.

edge_inputs.description: remove a consecutive pair leaving one letter
removing actually makes it larger, so keep it
wrap-around pair a and z cancels
nested removals collapse a long block

stress.description: short strings over a small alphabet including wrap letters so the BFS oracle stays fast
stress.note: small n keeps the exhaustive oracle tractable
